title: Bridging the Resource Gap: Deploying Advanced Imitation Learning Models onto Affordable Embedded Platforms
summary: Advanced imitation learning with structures like the transformer is increasingly demonstrating its advantages in robotics. However, deploying these large-scale models on embedded platforms remains a major challenge. In this paper, we propose a pipeline that facilitates the migration of advanced imitation learning algorithms to edge devices. The process is achieved via an efficient model compression method and a practical asynchronous parallel method Temporal Ensemble with Dropped Actions (TEDA) that enhances the smoothness of operations. To show the efficiency of the proposed pipeline, large-scale imitation learning models are trained on a server and deployed on an edge device to complete various manipulation tasks.
